concerns.text: Task T013 mandates: 'if N < 194, log warning, set power_warning flag, and proceed (do not halt)'. This directly contradicts spec.md User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 3 and Edge Case which require the system to 'halt execution and report a Power Insufficient error'. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical rigor constraint (FR-005/SC-003) allowing underpowered analysis.

Tasks T037-T039 require generating a 'Discussion: Cellular and Molecular Context' citing specific molecular mechanisms (CREB, synaptic efficacy) to address reviewer feedback. The spec.md explicitly limits the scope to an 'associative hypothesis' with no functional requirement for molecular validation or mechanistic discussion. This introduces scope creep that risks conflating macroscopic fMRI metrics with microscopic biology, violating the constitutional principle of staying within the defined associative scope.

Task T024 hardcodes the entropy tolerance parameter 'r=0.2' for the primary metric implementation. While T034 covers the sensitivity sweep, the spec (US-2, FR-006) defines the sensitivity sweep {0.1, 0.15, 0.2} as the method to determine robustness. Hardcoding a single value without explicit linkage to the sweep logic or a default selection rationale creates ambiguity about which value satisfies SC-004 (consistency across r) and risks implementing a static metric instead of the required robustness check.
